title: Cheque Bounce / Dishonoured Cheque
description: A cheque issued by a debtor has bounced due to insufficient funds or stopped payment. This is both a civil and criminal matter in India.
typical_solution: Send legal demand notice within 30 days of bounce. If no payment in 15 days, file criminal complaint under NI Act Section 138.
applicable_laws: Negotiable Instruments Act 1881, Section 138 (Dishonour of cheque — criminal)
Negotiable Instruments Act 1881, Section 142 (Cognizance of offences)
CrPC Section 200 (Complaint before Magistrate)
legal_process: Bounce → Bank memo → Legal notice within 30 days → Wait 15 days → Magistrate complaint → Trial → Max 2 years imprisonment + 2x fine
typical_timeline: Strict: Notice must go within 30 days of bounce; complaint within 30 days of notice expiry

title: Domestic Violence / Abuse by Spouse or Family
description: Woman facing physical, emotional, sexual, or economic abuse from husband or in-laws. May need immediate protection or relief.
typical_solution: File complaint with Protection Officer (every district has one). Approach Magistrate under DV Act. File FIR for physical violence under IPC.
applicable_laws: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act 2005, Section 12 (Application to Magistrate)
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act 2005, Section 18 (Protection orders)
IPC Section 498A (Husband or relative subjecting woman to cruelty)
IPC Section 323/324/325 (Hurt)
legal_process: Protection Officer → IR (Incident Report) → Magistrate application → Hearing → Protection/Residence/Maintenance order
typical_timeline: Protection order: within 3 days in urgent cases

title: Wife / Child Maintenance Not Paid
description: Separated or divorced woman or minor child not receiving court-ordered or agreed maintenance/alimony from husband/father.
typical_solution: File application under Section 125 CrPC in Magistrate court. File execution petition if already ordered but not paid.
applicable_laws: CrPC Section 125 (Order for maintenance of wives, children and parents)
Hindu Marriage Act 1955, Section 24 (Maintenance pendente lite)
Hindu Adoption and Maintenance Act 1956
Muslim Women (Protection of Rights on Divorce) Act 1986
legal_process: Application to Magistrate → Ex-parte interim maintenance possible → Full hearing → Order → Execution if defaulted
typical_timeline: Interim order possible in 1–3 months; final order 6–18 months

title: Stalking or Online Harassment
description: Person being followed, repeatedly contacted against their will, or harassed online (social media, WhatsApp, email) by known or unknown person.
typical_solution: File FIR at local police station or cyber cell. Apply for restraining order. Document all incidents with screenshots.
applicable_laws: IPC Section 354D (Stalking — added by Criminal Law Amendment 2013)
IT Act 2000, Section 67 (Obscene material online)
IT Act 2000, Section 66A — Note: Struck down but Section 66C/D applicable
BNS 2023, Section 78 (Stalking) — for offences after July 2024
legal_process: FIR → Cyber Cell investigation → Arrest → Trial → Up to 3 years imprisonment for first offence
typical_timeline: FIR must be filed immediately; bail-able for first offence

title: Illegal Loan App Harassment
description: Victim took loan from unlicensed app. App recovery agents calling contacts, sending morphed photos, threatening arrest, demanding excessive interest.
typical_solution: File cybercrime complaint. Complain to RBI Ombudsman. File FIR for extortion and defamation. Block app and delete.
applicable_laws: IT Act 2000, Section 66C (Identity theft of contacts data)
IT Act 2000, Section 66E (Privacy violation — sharing morphed images)
IPC Section 384 (Extortion)
IPC Section 499/500 (Defamation)
RBI Guidelines on Digital Lending (2022)
legal_process: Cybercrime portal complaint → RBI Ombudsman → FIR at cyber cell → Freeze fraudster accounts
typical_timeline: Report immediately; RBI Ombudsman responds in 30 days

title: Insurance Claim Wrongly Rejected
description: Insurance company rejecting health, motor, or life insurance claim without valid reason or citing exclusions that were never disclosed at time of purchase.
typical_solution: Send legal notice to insurer. File complaint with IRDAI Bima Bharosa portal. Approach Insurance Ombudsman. Consumer Forum also available.
applicable_laws: Insurance Act 1938, Section 45 (No repudiation after 3 years)
Consumer Protection Act 2019 (Deficiency of service)
IRDAI (Protection of Policyholders Interests) Regulations 2017
legal_process: Complaint to insurer → Legal notice → IRDAI Bima Bharosa portal → Insurance Ombudsman → Consumer Forum

title: Property Document Fraud / Benami Transaction
description: Someone forged property documents, sold property without owner's knowledge, or property held in benami name causing disputes.
typical_solution: Lodge FIR for document forgery. File complaint with Registrar of Properties. Seek injunction from Civil Court.
applicable_laws: IPC Section 420 (Cheating)
IPC Section 467 (Forgery of valuable security)
IPC Section 468 (Forgery for purpose of cheating)
Benami Transactions (Prohibition) Amendment Act 2016
Transfer of Property Act 1882
legal_process: FIR → Investigation → Civil suit for injunction → Declaration of title → Recovery
typical_timeline: Civil suits take 2–5 years; criminal cases 1–3 years

title: Sexual Harassment at Workplace (POSH)
description: Employee facing sexual harassment from colleague or superior. Company may not have Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) or may be suppressing complaint.
typical_solution: File complaint with ICC within 3 months. If no ICC, file with Local Complaints Committee (LCC) at District level. File FIR if criminal conduct.
applicable_laws: Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace Act 2013 (POSH Act), Section 9 (Complaint)
POSH Act Section 11 (Inquiry into complaint)
IPC Section 354A (Sexual harassment)
IPC Section 354 (Assault on woman)
legal_process: ICC complaint → Conciliation attempt (optional) → Inquiry → Report to employer → Action within 60 days
typical_timeline: ICC must complete inquiry in 90 days

title: Personal Loan Given to Friend / Relative Not Returned
description: Victim lent money to known person on trust. Borrower now denying loan or refusing to repay despite repeated requests.
typical_solution: Send legal notice. File civil suit for recovery in appropriate court. If cheque was given and bounced, use NI Act Section 138.
applicable_laws: Code of Civil Procedure 1908, Order 37 (Summary suit for money)
Limitation Act 1963, Section 18 (Acknowledgement of debt extends limitation to 3 years)
Evidence Act 1872, Section 118 (Presumption of consideration in promissory note)
legal_process: Legal notice → Civil summary suit → Ex-parte decree if no reply → Execution → Recovery

title: College / Coaching Institute Refusing Fee Refund
description: Student cancelled admission or left course but institute refusing to refund fees, citing no-refund policy in violation of UGC/state regulations.
typical_solution: Send legal notice citing UGC refund policy. File consumer complaint at DCDRC. File complaint with UGC/state education regulator.
applicable_laws: UGC (Refund of Fees) Guidelines 2018 (Pro-rata refund based on withdrawal date)
Consumer Protection Act 2019, Section 2(42) (Unfair trade practice — no-refund policy)
State fee regulation acts (vary by state)
legal_process: Legal notice → UGC complaint → Consumer Forum → Order for refund + compensation

title: Unauthorized Bank Transaction / Card Fraud
description: Money debited from bank account or credit card without customer's knowledge. May be card skimming, phishing, or insider fraud.
typical_solution: Immediately block card. Report to bank within 3 days for zero liability. File complaint on cybercrime.gov.in and with banking ombudsman.
applicable_laws: RBI Circular on Customer Protection in Unauthorised Electronic Banking Transactions 2017
IT Act 2000, Section 43 (Penalty for damage to computer system)
IT Act 2000, Section 66C (Identity theft)
legal_process: Bank complaint (written) → Cybercrime portal → RBI Banking Ombudsman if bank rejects → Consumer Forum
typical_timeline: Zero liability if reported within 3 days; bank must resolve in 90 days
